Gene expression profiling and predictive modeling of cancer biomarkers using machine learning and IoT-Enabled biosensors

evidence_entities.text: Cancer is a leading cause of global mortality, accounting for nearly 10 million deaths annually.
CNN achieved the highest accuracy (97.2%) using stratified 5-fold cross-validation, precision-recall-balanced evaluation, and dropout-based regularization to reduce overfitting.
Wearable biosensors detected CEA (-0.133 V) and HER2 (-0.130 V) efficiently, while implantable biosensors identified AFP (0.26 A) with high optical absorbance.
STRING analysis revealed HER2 and AKT1 as key hub genes involved in PI3K-Akt, JAK-STAT, and MAPK signaling pathways, which play critical roles in tumor proliferation and metastasis.
enrichment analysis also identified underreported processes such as viral carcinogenesis and immune checkpoint dysregulation, offering additional translational insight.
